title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Recovery Case: Undo Layer Choice
summary: Recover from an incorrect edit or commit using the least destructive operation and a verified recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git undo layer choice increment in an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.
Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Undo Layer Choice
steps.instruction:
theory case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Undo Layer Choice contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.

content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Undo Layer Choice condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

steps.title: Bound Undo Layer Choice
steps.instruction:
theory case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.

content.text: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer. A defensible solution applies Undo Layer Choice, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

content.code:
git status --short
git branch recovery/before-repair
git diff
git reflog -n 7
# Choose restore, revert, or reset only after naming the affected layer.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake. This fails the Undo Layer Choice contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Undo Layer Choice evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-undo-layer-choice-w39clz" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Undo Layer Choice into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Repair the Undo Layer Choice defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

A teammate acts on this belief: “There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.” Which repair addresses the cause?
